methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Lace Lithography is a Series A stage company in Chips & Semiconductors that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Etched, Substrate, Positron AI, Rain AI) alongside 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors on the $60M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A progression, implying $120M. Peer-median triangulation uses four contemporaneous private rounds in semiconductors and AI infrastructure, yielding a $1.0B median valuation with a $500M to $5.0B range. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 20.3x) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which remain unavailable for the private target.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $102M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $560M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty: stage-progression is methodologically conservative, while peer-median captures recent capital intensity and investor appetite in frontier semiconductor infrastructure. Key sensitivities include revenue trajectory relative to peers, technology risk, and macroeconomic conditions affecting downstream chip design cycles. The median estimate of $560M sits between purely stage-mechanical approaches and full peer-set valuation, appropriate for a company with undisclosed traction metrics.
